name: Tenant-to-conversation-group authorization
type: Shared Core authorization / Portal identity / module data guards

dataIsolation.guard: Every backend request is tenant-locked by notionRequest and every Portal-owned record is additionally checked against its group-binding relation before read, PATCH, batch processing, or attachment access.

definitionOfDone: Portal stores reviewed amAccess mode all or selected group-binding page IDs and authzVersion 3 without new-account defaults
Permission changes are validated against the server-side AM directory and appended to am_authz_audit
Core accepts only public, machine, tenant, or group module routes and creates a live AccessContext for tenant/group routes
Groups, queue, tasks, and group-owned construction tickets enforce per-record group relations
Every enabled module is covered by public, machine, tenant, group, or system-principal policy and meeting-created tasks retain their source group
Migrating AM systems remain visible in the Portal directory but cannot be assigned until their group-binding and isolation setup is verifiably ready
Opaque AM sessions reload the active Portal user on every Core request
Cross-group page ID, attachment ID, and batch dry-runs produce no unauthorized Notion PATCH
Forest, engineering, HOZO_AM, and SevenAM are reviewed and verified separately before enforce mode
